purpose: Canonical MCP server contract for AgentCore IDE clients. Use this to verify mandatory and essential MCP servers, client-specific surfaces, retired routes, and unique setup requirements without exposing secrets.

normal_memory_rule.swarmrecall_mcp_launch: governed wrapper ops\Invoke-AgentCoreSwarmRecall.ps1 -Mode Mcp (local-only; strips Upstash/Firebase; credentials from Windows env)
normal_memory_rule.swarmvault_mcp_launch: governed wrapper ops\Invoke-AgentCoreSwarmVault.ps1 -Mode Mcp (local-first file vault at F:\AgentCore\agentmemory\swarmvault)
normal_memory_rule.swarmrecall_backend_role: shared local memory runtime and retrieval service; now a mandatory baseline MCP surface (local-only)
normal_memory_rule.swarmvault_backend_role: shared local RAG/wiki substrate; now a mandatory baseline MCP surface (local-first files)
normal_memory_rule.projection_model: control-plane projector (Invoke-AgentCoreMemoryProjector.ps1) mirrors approved governed memory and curated knowledge downstream; normal durable task-end memory still routes through global-memory-gateway

global-memory-gateway.role: governed durable memory write and search path

artiforge.role: codebase orchestration, project docs, and architecture-sensitive planning

sequential-thinking.role: approved reasoning/planning MCP; do not restore thinking-patterns

serena.role: repo navigation, symbol understanding, diagnostics, and refactors

context-fabric.role: Git-backed project context and drift checks; not durable memory

filesystem.role: bounded filesystem access for IDEs that need direct file tooling

obsidian-vault.role: long-form notes, decisions, runbooks, and indexed handoffs

playwright.role: local browser automation and UI verification

github-mcp.role: GitHub repository, issue, PR, and remote workflow access

cursor-agent-mcp.role: Cursor agent bridge where Cursor/OpenClaw workflows need it

mcp-debugger.role: MCP debug tooling for clients with larger approved surfaces

notes: Preserve in OpenClaw renderer and live config.
Do not copy into Codex, Cursor, MiniMax, Mavis, or Open Interpreter.

swarmrecall.backends: local PostgreSQL `swarmrecall` DB (127.0.0.1:55432, separate from agent_core) + Meilisearch (127.0.0.1:7700)
notes: Governed wrapper forces local-only env and reads SwarmRecall API key + Meili master key from Windows environment variables.
Do not emit any hosted SwarmRecall URL (e.g. *.onrender.com).

Fallback per plan: if the wrapper fails stdio MCP discovery, the documented fallback is the vendored CLI `node ...\swarmrecall\packages\cli\dist\index.js mcp` with local-only env (SWARMRECALL_API_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3300).

tool_profiles.swarmvault-admin: Codex, Cursor (full or near-full tool surface)
tool_profiles.swarmvault-lite: OpenClaw, Open Interpreter, MiniMax, Mavis, Antigravity, Claude Code (read/query/context/task-health subset)

notes: Not a Postgres database. Local-first file vault; offline heuristic provider; no hosted/cloud persistence.
Replaces the rogue-added broken reference to C:\Users\ynotf\.agentcore\mcp-wrappers\swarmvault-mcp.ps1.

codex-security.role: Codex security plugin tools; enabled only when available through Codex plugin cache

node_repl.role: Codex desktop runtime integration and browser/control helper

server_budget.reason: Codex context pressure is the tightest; optional direct app connectors should be installed only when the active task needs them.

unique_notes: GitHub appears as the Codex remote server name `github`, not `github-mcp`.
node_repl and codex-security are Codex-managed and should not be copied into other IDE JSON files.
Direct Firecrawl routes are not part of the default AgentCore memory rollout surface.

server_budget.reason: Cursor keeps core repo/code and browser tooling by default; context-fabric, cursor-agent-mcp, github-mcp, and mcp-debugger are task-specific opt-ins.

unique_notes: Keep `context-fabric`, `cursor-agent-mcp`, `github-mcp`, and `mcp-debugger` off the default surface unless the active task needs that route.

server_budget.reason: OpenClaw keeps the same reduced default surface as Cursor plus the approved `eye2byte` exception.

unique_notes: `eye2byte` is a user-approved OpenClaw-only exception and is part of the default OpenClaw renderer surface.
Do not remove `eye2byte` as drift unless the user explicitly retires it.
Keep `context-fabric`, `cursor-agent-mcp`, `github-mcp`, and `mcp-debugger` off the default surface unless the active task needs that route.
Keep this surface small. Interpreter stores profile and auth state in the same app data tree, so live config edits must preserve unrelated top-level config fields.
MCP server list lives under mcpServers.
Mavis uses the MiniMax server surface but has a separate live config path.

server_budget.reason: Antigravity uses the same bounded code-IDE surface as Cursor; GitKraken and other Antigravity-specific servers stay disabled or quarantined unless explicitly approved.

unique_notes: Official Antigravity MCP configuration uses a top-level `mcpServers` object.
Primary managed config on this PC is `C:\Users\ynotf\.gemini\config\mcp_config.json`; the Roaming Antigravity User mcp.json is maintained as a secondary compatibility target.
Existing GitKraken, context7, cloudrun, firebase, and raw GitHub-token servers are not part of the default AgentCore surface.

notes: context7 is retired in favor of arabold-docs.
raw Mem0 is replaced by global-memory-gateway for normal memory.
Composio remains quarantined until a stable MCP/OAuth path is explicitly approved.
SwarmRecall stays backend/local runtime in v1; direct broad MCP exposure is intentionally absent.

validation.post_config_change_requirement: Restart affected clients before claiming adoption.
